messages.content: Write a Manim script to visualize: Show a Hadamard gate being applied to a qubit in state |0>, illustrating the qubit state before and after the operation.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm HadamardGateBlochSphere

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class HadamardGateBlochSphere(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=70 * DEGREES, theta=30 * DEGREES)

        title = Text("Hadamard Gate: |0> -> |+>", font_size=28)
        title.to_corner(UL)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)

        sphere = Sphere(radius=2, resolution=(24, 24), fill_opacity=0.12,
                         checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, BLUE_E])
        axes = ThreeDAxes()
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(sphere))

        z_up = MathTex(r"|0\rangle").move_to([0, 0, 2.4])
        z_down = MathTex(r"|1\rangle").move_to([0, 0, -2.4])
        x_plus = MathTex(r"|+\rangle").move_to([2.4, 0, 0])
        self.add_fixed_orientation_mobjects(z_up, z_down, x_plus)

        state_label = Text("Before: |0>", font_size=22, color=YELLOW).to_corner(UR)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(state_label)

        theta_tracker = ValueTracker(0)

        def bloch_vector():
            th = theta_tracker.get_value()
            point = 2 * np.array([np.sin(th), 0, np.cos(th)])
            return Arrow3D(start=ORIGIN, end=point, color=YELLOW)

        vector = always_redraw(bloch_vector)
        self.add(vector)

        formula = MathTex(r"H|0\rangle=\tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}(|0\rangle+|1\rangle)=|+\rangle", font_size=26).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(formula)

        self.wait(1)
        after_label = Text("After: |+>", font_size=22, color=GREEN).to_corner(UR)
        self.play(theta_tracker.animate.set_value(PI / 2), run_time=3, rate_func=smooth)
        self.play(Transform(state_label, after_label))
        self.wait(2)

```

Write a Manim script to visualize: Show a CNOT gate acting on a Hadamard-prepared control qubit and a |0> target qubit, creating a Bell state.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm CNOTBellState

from manim import *

class CNOTBellState(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("CNOT Gate Creates a Bell State", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        q0_line = Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=WHITE).shift(UP * 1)
        q1_line = Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=WHITE).shift(DOWN * 1)
        q0_label = MathTex("|0\\rangle").next_to(q0_line, LEFT)
        q1_label = MathTex("|0\\rangle").next_to(q1_line, LEFT)
        self.play(Create(q0_line), Create(q1_line), Write(q0_label), Write(q1_label))

        h_box = Square(side_length=0.6, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.6).move_to(LEFT * 2 + UP * 1)
        h_label = MathTex("H", font_size=26).move_to(h_box)
        self.play(Create(h_box), Write(h_label))

        control_dot = Dot(RIGHT * 1 + UP * 1, color=YELLOW, radius=0.12)
        target_circle = Circle(radius=0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(RIGHT * 1 + DOWN * 1)
        target_plus_v = Line(UP * 0.3, DOWN * 0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(target_circle.get_center())
        target_plus_h = Line(LEFT * 0.3, RIGHT * 0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(target_circle.get_center())
        connector = Line(control_dot.get_center(), target_circle.get_center(), color=YELLOW)
        self.play(Create(connector), FadeIn(control_dot), Create(target_circle), Create(target_plus_v), Create(target_plus_h))

        before = MathTex(r"|00\rangle", font_size=28).to_corner(UL).shift(DOWN)
        after = MathTex(r"\tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}(|00\rangle+|11\rangle)", font_size=28).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(before))
        self.play(Write(after))

        note = Text("Entangled: measuring q0 instantly determines q1's outcome", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)

```
Write a Manim script to visualize: Show measurement of a Bell pair, illustrating how the two qubits always give correlated outcomes.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm BellMeasurementCorrelations

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class BellMeasurementCorrelations(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Measuring a Bell Pair: Correlated Outcomes", font_size=27).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        state = MathTex(r"|\psi\rangle=\tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}(|00\rangle+|11\rangle)", font_size=30).shift(UP * 1.8)
        self.play(Write(state))

        qA = Circle(radius=0.5, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3).shift(LEFT * 3)
        qB = Circle(radius=0.5, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3).shift(RIGHT * 3)
        link = Line(qA.get_right(), qB.get_left(), color=YELLOW)
        labelA = Text("Qubit A", font_size=18).next_to(qA, DOWN)
        labelB = Text("Qubit B", font_size=18).next_to(qB, DOWN)
        self.play(Create(qA), Create(qB), Create(link), Write(labelA), Write(labelB))

        measureA = Text("Measure A -> 0", font_size=20, color=GREEN).next_to(qA, UP)
        self.play(Write(measureA))
        self.play(qA.animate.set_fill(GREEN, opacity=0.8))

        measureB = Text("Measure B -> always 0", font_size=20, color=GREEN).next_to(qB, UP)
        self.play(Write(measureB))
        self.play(qB.animate.set_fill(GREEN, opacity=0.8))

        note = Text("Outcomes are perfectly correlated, even though each qubit's result is individually random", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)

```
Write a Manim script to visualize: Show the creation of a three-qubit GHZ state using a Hadamard gate and two CNOT gates.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm GHZStateCreation

from manim import *

class GHZStateCreation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Creating a GHZ State: H + CNOT + CNOT", font_size=27).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        lines = VGroup(*[
            Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=WHITE).shift(UP * (1.2 - i * 1.2)) for i in range(3)
        ])
        labels = VGroup(*[
            MathTex("|0\\rangle").next_to(lines[i], LEFT) for i in range(3)
        ])
        self.play(*[Create(l) for l in lines], *[Write(lb) for lb in labels])

        h_box = Square(side_length=0.6, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.6).move_to(LEFT * 3 + UP * 1.2)
        h_label = MathTex("H", font_size=26).move_to(h_box)
        self.play(Create(h_box), Write(h_label))

        c1 = Dot(LEFT * 1 + UP * 1.2, color=YELLOW, radius=0.12)
        t1 = Circle(radius=0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(LEFT * 1)
        conn1 = Line(c1.get_center(), t1.get_center(), color=YELLOW)
        self.play(Create(conn1), FadeIn(c1), Create(t1))

        c2 = Dot(RIGHT * 1, color=YELLOW, radius=0.12)
        t2 = Circle(radius=0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(RIGHT * 1 + DOWN * 1.2)
        conn2 = Line(c2.get_center(), t2.get_center(), color=YELLOW)
        self.play(Create(conn2), FadeIn(c2), Create(t2))

        final_state = MathTex(r"\tfrac{1}{\sqrt2}(|000\rangle+|111\rangle)", font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN).shift(UP * 0.6)
        self.play(Write(final_state))

        note = Text("All three qubits are now maximally entangled together", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)

```
Write a Manim script to visualize: Show the quantum teleportation protocol, transferring an unknown qubit state using an entangled pair and classical bits.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm QuantumTeleportationProtocol

from manim import *

class QuantumTeleportationProtocol(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Quantum Teleportation Protocol", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        alice_q = MathTex(r"|\psi\rangle", font_size=26).move_to(LEFT * 5 + UP * 1.6)
        alice_e = MathTex(r"|0\rangle", font_size=26).move_to(LEFT * 5 + UP * 0)
        bob_e = MathTex(r"|0\rangle", font_size=26).move_to(LEFT * 5 + DOWN * 1.6)
        self.play(Write(alice_q), Write(alice_e), Write(bob_e))

        entangle_label = Text("1. Create entangled pair (H + CNOT)", font_size=18).shift(UP * 2.6 + LEFT*1)
        self.play(Write(entangle_label))
        bell_link = Line(LEFT * 3.5 + UP * 0, RIGHT * 0 + DOWN * 1.6, color=YELLOW)
        self.play(Create(bell_link))

        bell_measure_label = Text("2. Alice performs Bell measurement on her two qubits", font_size=18).next_to(entangle_label, DOWN)
        self.play(Write(bell_measure_label))

        classical_arrow = Arrow(LEFT * 2 + UP*0.3, RIGHT * 1.5 + DOWN * 1.4, color=GREEN)
        classical_label = Text("3. Send 2 classical bits", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(classical_arrow, RIGHT).shift(UP*0.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(classical_arrow), Write(classical_label))

        correction_label = Text("4. Bob applies correction (I, X, Z, or XZ)", font_size=18).next_to(bell_measure_label, DOWN)
        self.play(Write(correction_label))

        result = MathTex(r"|\psi\rangle", font_size=30, color=YELLOW).move_to(RIGHT * 4 + DOWN * 1.6)
        self.play(TransformFromCopy(alice_q, result))

        note = Text("The original qubit state is now at Bob's location; no cloning occurred", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)

```

Write a Manim script to visualize: Show why the no-cloning theorem prevents an unknown quantum state from being perfectly copied.

To visualize this concept, we can use the Manim library to animate the gate acting on the qubit's before and after states... 

```python
%%manim -qm NoCloningTheorem

from manim import *

class NoCloningTheorem(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("The No-Cloning Theorem", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        original = MathTex(r"|\psi\rangle", font_size=36, color=YELLOW).shift(LEFT * 3)
        blank = MathTex(r"|0\rangle", font_size=36).shift(RIGHT * 3)
        self.play(Write(original), Write(blank))

        attempt_box = Rectangle(width=2.4, height=1.4, color=RED)
        attempt_label = Text("Cloning\nMachine?", font_size=20, color=RED).move_to(attempt_box)
        self.play(Create(attempt_box), Write(attempt_label))

        equation = MathTex(
            r"U(|\psi\rangle|0\rangle) \overset{?}{=} |\psi\rangle|\psi\rangle",
            font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN).shift(UP * 1.2)
        self.play(Write(equation))

        contradiction = MathTex(
            r"\text{Linearity forces } U(\alpha|0\rangle+\beta|1\rangle)|0\rangle \ne (\alpha|0\rangle+\beta|1\rangle)^{\otimes 2}",
            font_size=24).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(contradiction))

        cross = Cross(attempt_box, color=RED, stroke_width=6)
        self.play(Create(cross))

        note = Text("No unitary U can clone an arbitrary unknown state", font_size=20).next_to(attempt_box, UP)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)

```

```python
from manim import *

class TunnelingProblems(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Quantum Tunneling Through a Potential Barrier", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        axes = Axes(x_range=[-6, 6, 1], y_range=[0, 3, 1], x_length=10, y_length=4,
                     axis_config={"include_tip": False}).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        def barrier(x):
            return 2 if -1 <= x <= 1 else 0

        barrier_graph = axes.plot(barrier, x_range=[-6, 6], use_smoothing=False, color=RED)
        barrier_label = Text("V(x)", font_size=24, color=RED).next_to(barrier_graph, UP, buff=0.3).shift(LEFT * 3)
```

```python
%%manim -qm HermitePolynomialSolutions

from manim import *
import numpy as np
from numpy.polynomial.hermite import hermval

class HermitePolynomialSolutions(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Harmonic Oscillator Eigenstates via Hermite Polynomials", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        axes = Axes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[0, 6, 1], x_length=10, y_length=5.5).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        potential = axes.plot(lambda x: 0.3 * x ** 2, x_range=[-4, 4], color=GREY)
        self.play(Create(potential))

        colors = [BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW, RED]
        curves = VGroup()
        labels = VGroup()

        for n in range(4):
            coeffs = [0] * n + [1]
            energy_offset = n + 0.5

            def psi_n(x, n=n, coeffs=coeffs, offset=energy_offset):
                herm = hermval(x, coeffs)
                gaussian = np.exp(-x ** 2 / 2)
                return 0.4 * herm * gaussian + offset

            curve = axes.plot(lambda x, n=n: psi_n(x), x_range=[-4, 4], color=colors[n])
            label = MathTex(f"n={n}", color=colors[n], font_size=24).next_to(
                axes.c2p(3.5, energy_offset), RIGHT)
            curves.add(curve)
            labels.add(label)

        for c, l in zip(curves, labels):
            self.play(Create(c), Write(l), run_time=1)

        self.wait(2)
```

```python
class WavePacketSpreading(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Gaussian Wave Packet Spreading Over Time", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        axes = Axes(x_range=[-6, 6, 1], y_range=[0, 1.2, 1], x_length=11, y_length=5).shift(DOWN * 0.3)

            lambda x: packet(x, t_tracker.get_value()), x_range=[-6, 6], color=BLUE))
        self.add(curve)

        formula = MathTex(r"\sigma(t) = \sigma_0\sqrt{1+\left(\frac{\hbar t}{2m\sigma_0^2}\right)^2}", font_size=26).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        self.play(t_tracker.animate.set_value(8), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait()
```

```python
class BerryPhase(ThreeDScene):

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=65 * DEGREES, theta=30 * DEGREES)

        title.to_corner(UL)

        axes = ThreeDAxes()

            t_range=[0, TAU], color=YELLOW)
        self.play(Create(loop))

        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(formula)

        self.play(t_tracker.animate.set_value(TAU), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
```

```python
        self.play(Write(angle_label))

        formula = MathTex(r"\frac{d\sigma}{d\Omega} = \left(\frac{Z_1 Z_2 e^2}{4E}\right)^2 \frac{1}{\sin^4(\theta/2)}",
```

```python
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class PhononTheory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Phonons: Quantized Lattice Vibrations", font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        n_atoms = 9
        spacing = 0.9
        atoms = VGroup(*[
            Dot(LEFT * (n_atoms - 1) / 2 * spacing + RIGHT * i * spacing, color=BLUE, radius=0.15)
            for i in range(n_atoms)
        ])
        self.play(*[FadeIn(a) for a in atoms])

        springs = VGroup(*[
            Line(atoms[i].get_center(), atoms[i+1].get_center(), color=GREY)
            for i in range(n_atoms - 1)
        ])
        self.play(*[Create(s) for s in springs])

        t_tracker = ValueTracker(0)
        k_wave = 1.2

        def update_atoms(mob):
            for i, atom in enumerate(mob):
                x0 = (-(n_atoms - 1) / 2 + i) * spacing
                displacement = 0.15 * np.sin(k_wave * x0 - 2 * t_tracker.get_value())
                atom.move_to([x0 + displacement, 0, 0])

        atoms.add_updater(update_atoms)

        def update_springs(mob):
            for i, spring in enumerate(mob):
                spring.put_start_and_end_on(atoms[i].get_center(), atoms[i+1].get_center())

        springs.add_updater(update_springs)

        formula = MathTex(r"E_n = \left(n+\tfrac12\right)\hbar\omega_k", font_size=30).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        self.play(t_tracker.animate.set_value(TAU * 2), run_time=6, rate_func=linear)
        atoms.clear_updaters()
        springs.clear_updaters()
        self.wait()
```

```python
        nucleus = Dot(ORIGIN, color=RED, radius=0.2)
        nucleus_label = Text("Nucleus", font_size=16, color=RED).next_to(nucleus, DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(nucleus), Write(nucleus_label))

        electron_clouds = VGroup(*[
            Circle(radius=1 + 0.6*i, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.15).move_to(ORIGIN)
            for i in range(3)
        ])
        self.play(*[Create(c) for c in electron_clouds])

        cloud_label = Text("Each electron feels the AVERAGED field of all others", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(cloud_label))

        equation = MathTex(r"\hat{F}\,\phi_i = \varepsilon_i\,\phi_i", font_size=30).shift(UP * 2.5)
        self.play(Write(equation))

        note = Text("Self-consistent field (SCF): solve, update field, repeat until converged", font_size=18).next_to(cloud_label, UP)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)
```

```python
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class VibrationalSpectroscopy(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Vibrational Spectroscopy: Morse Potential Energy Levels", font_size=25).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 5, 1], y_range=[0, 5, 1], x_length=9, y_length=5.5).shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        self.play(Create(axes))

        def morse(r, D=4, a=1, r0=2):
            return D * (1 - np.exp(-a * (r - r0))) ** 2

        curve = axes.plot(morse, x_range=[0.7, 4.5], color=GREY)
        self.play(Create(curve))

        for n in range(4):
            E = (n + 0.5) - 0.1 * (n + 0.5) ** 2
            r_left = 2 - np.sqrt(-np.log(1 - np.sqrt(E/4))) if E < 4 else 1
            level = axes.plot(lambda r, E=E: E if abs(morse(r) - E) < 10 else None, x_range=[1, 3.5], color=BLUE)
            hline = DashedLine(axes.c2p(1.2, E), axes.c2p(3.6 - n*0.15, E), color=[BLUE,GREEN,YELLOW,RED][n])
            label = MathTex(f"v={n}", font_size=20, color=[BLUE,GREEN,YELLOW,RED][n]).next_to(hline, RIGHT)
            self.play(Create(hline), Write(label), run_time=0.7)

        note = Text("Levels converge near dissociation energy D -- anharmonic spacing", font_size=18).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(note))
        self.wait(2)
```

```python
%%manim -qm ThermalStates

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ThermalStates(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Thermal States: Boltzmann Population of Energy Levels", font_size=26).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 5, 1], y_range=[0, 1, 0.5], x_length=9, y_length=4.5).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        y_label = Text("Population", font_size=18).next_to(axes.y_axis, LEFT).rotate(PI/2)
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(y_label))

        n_levels = 5
        bars = VGroup()
        beta_tracker = ValueTracker(0.3)

        def populations(beta):
            energies = np.arange(n_levels)
            weights = np.exp(-beta * energies)
            return weights / weights.sum()

        for i in range(n_levels):
            bar = always_redraw(lambda i=i: Rectangle(
                width=0.7, height=4.5 * populations(beta_tracker.get_value())[i],
                color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.7
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN))
            bars.add(bar)
        self.add(bars)

        formula = MathTex(r"\rho = \frac{e^{-\beta H}}{Z},\quad P_n \propto e^{-\beta E_n}", font_size=28).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        note = Text("Higher temperature (smaller β) populates more excited states", font_size=18).to_corner(UR)
        self.play(Write(note))

        self.play(beta_tracker.animate.set_value(2.0), run_time=3)
        self.play(beta_tracker.animate.set_value(0.1), run_time=3)
        self.wait()

```

```python
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class MomentumFourierTransform(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Position and Momentum Wavefunctions as Fourier Pairs", font_size=25).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))

        x_axes = Axes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[0, 1.2, 1], x_length=5.5, y_length=3.5).shift(LEFT * 3 + DOWN * 0.7)
        p_axes = Axes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[0, 1.2, 1], x_length=5.5, y_length=3.5).shift(RIGHT * 3 + DOWN * 0.7)
        x_title = Text("ψ(x)", font_size=20).next_to(x_axes, UP)
        p_title = Text("φ(p) = FT[ψ(x)]", font_size=20).next_to(p_axes, UP)
        self.play(Create(x_axes), Create(p_axes), Write(x_title), Write(p_title))

        width_tracker = ValueTracker(1.0)

        x_curve = always_redraw(lambda: x_axes.plot(
            lambda x: np.exp(-x ** 2 / (2 * width_tracker.get_value() ** 2)), color=BLUE))
        p_curve = always_redraw(lambda: p_axes.plot(
            lambda p: np.exp(-p ** 2 * width_tracker.get_value() ** 2 / 2), color=RED))
        self.add(x_curve, p_curve)

        formula = MathTex(r"\phi(p) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi\hbar}}\int \psi(x) e^{-ipx/\hbar}\,dx",
                           font_size=24).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        self.play(width_tracker.animate.set_value(0.4), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(width_tracker.animate.set_value(2.0), run_time=3)
        self.wait()
```
